zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret De Braose於1262年出生在英國諾福爾可時，她的父親Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor31歲，母親Alice le Rus17歲。她於1286年之前與Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1335年5月12日在英國林可爾史爾卡素 白坦享年73歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret De Braose于1262年出生在英国諾福爾可时，她的父亲Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor31岁，母亲Alice le Rus17岁。她于1286年之前与Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1335年5月12日在英国林可爾史爾卡素 白坦享年73岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Braose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Eckhard, Gerhard, Manfred, Otto.
from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Ambrosius (see Ambrose ).

variants.name: Ambrose
meanings: English and Scottish: from the personal name Ambrose (French Ambroise, Latin Ambrosius, from Greek ambrosios ‘immortal, divine’), which was popular throughout Christendom in medieval Europe. Its popularity was due in part to the fame of Saint Ambrose (c. 340–397), one of the four Latin Fathers of the Church, the teacher of Saint Augustine. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Czech, Slovenian, and Croatian Ambrož (see Ambroz ), and also their derivatives, e.g. the Slovenian patronymic Ambrožič.
Irish: from Mac Ambróis ‘son of Ambrose’ (see 1 above); a West Munster name, which has also been Anglicized as McCambridge .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret De Braose was born in 1262, in Norfolk, England, her father, Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor, was 31 and her mother, Alice le Rus, was 17. She married Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville before 1286. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 12 May 1335, in Castle Bytham, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 73.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret De Braose est née en 1262, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Alice le Rus, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville avant 1286. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 12 mai 1335, à Castle Bytham, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 73 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret De Braose 1262 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor 31 und ihre Mutter Alice le Rus 17. Sie hat vor 1286 Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 12. Mai 1335 im Alter von 73 Jahren in Castle Bytham, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret De Braose è nata il nel 1262 nel Norfolk, England, sua padre, Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Alice le Rus, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville prima del 1286. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 12 maggio 1335, nel Castle Bytham, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 73 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret De Braoseが1262年にイギリスノーフォルクで生まれた時，父Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manorは31歳，母Alice le Rusは17歳であった。1286年以前にRoger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colvilleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1335年5月12日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアカストル ブイサムで73歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret De Braose(이)가 1262년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice le Rus(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1286년 이전 Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1335년 5월 12일 영국 링코실르 카스틀 비섬에서 73세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret De Braose nasceu em 1262, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Alice le Rus, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville antes 1286. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 12 de maio de 1335, em Castle Bytham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 73 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret De Braose родилась в 1262 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Alice le Rus, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville перед 1286 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 12 мая 1335 г. в местности кастл битам, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 73 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret De Braose nació en 1262, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Braose Lord Stinton Manor, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Alice le Rus, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Roger de Coleville 2nd Baron Colville antes de 1286. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 12 de mayo de 1335, en Castle Bytham, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 73 años.
